text.title: Ad Account Hygiene Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every application of Ad Account Hygiene Checklist MUST enforce. Rationale + source per rule. Rules referenced by id from `content/06-decision-tree.xml`.

rule.statement: Checklist MUST partition items into weekly (14 items) and monthly (8 items) cadences. Mixed-cadence checklists are skipped.
rule.rationale: Items with mixed cadences confuse owners; explicit splits create a runnable ritual.

rule.statement: Every item MUST tag platform (Google / Meta / LinkedIn / other). Cross-platform checklists invite skips.
rule.rationale: Platform-specific items skipped silently when not tagged; tagging surfaces them in audits.

rule.statement: Each completed item MUST log evidence (timestamp + reviewer + observation). Self-attestation alone is rejected.
rule.rationale: Self-attestation without evidence fails audits; evidence anchors trust.

rule.statement: Every item MUST have a named owner. Un-owned items default to 'no one' and skip silently.
rule.rationale: Checklists with shared ownership collapse; explicit owner is the only enforcement.

rule.statement: Skipped items MUST log a written reason in the audit log. Blank skips invalidate the cycle.
rule.rationale: Silent skips are how drift creeps in; reasons surface patterns over quarters.

rule.statement: If applicability preconditions are unmet (see AGENTS.md Skip If), the methodology MUST be skipped and a one-line skip-reason logged.
rule.rationale: A named skip terminal keeps decision-tree leaves resolvable and prevents silent misuse on misfit inputs.
